title: Sphalanthus pellegrinianus

intro: Sphalanthus pellegrinianus, also known as Pellegrin's Sphalanthus, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and aromatic leaves. Traditionally, Sphalanthus pellegrinianus has been used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sphalanthus pellegrinianus is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for the treatment of various skin conditions.
